name: Andreea Musat
bio: A certified NLP coach and artist with 5 years running workshops and art classes for kids. She blends creative instinct with structured thinking, helping students find their footing, take creative risks, and discover they're capable of more than they imagined.

items.title: Think like a Scientist
items.desc: Run real chemistry experiments at home until results are clean and repeatable — then prove it to a real scientist live.

items.title: Sound the Alarm
items.desc: Build a real AI agent that listens, recognizes a signal, and sends alerts.

items.title: The Peace Table
items.desc: Learn to work together and resolve conflicts — listen, understand both sides, and earn a Peace-Maker badge.

items.title: Sold!
items.desc: Write, shoot, and edit a real commercial, then screen it to a live audience who decide: would we buy it?

items.title: Hidden Stories
items.desc: Become a documentary filmmaker: find a story, shoot and edit it, and premiere it at a real film festival.

name: Anjelina Belakovskaia
bio: A 3-Time U.S. Women’s Chess Champion, Woman Grandmaster, finance professor, and former derivatives trader. Trained at the Botvinnik-Kasparov Chess Academy, she helps students develop critical thinking, creativity, confidence, and resilience through chess.

items.title: Chess Foundations
items.desc: Recreational chess for beginners: rules, piece movement, and core tactics in a fun, low-pressure setting.

items.title: Chess Mastery
items.desc: Advanced recreational players deepen positional understanding and competitive skill.

items.title: Strategic Chess
items.desc: Developing players sharpen strategy, tactics, and game planning.

items.title: Competitive Chess
items.desc: USCF-rated competitive chess across five levels — Rookies, Intermediate, Advanced, National, Grandmaster — matched to each player’s rating.

name: Craig Lundberg
bio: An educator, former principal, coach, and learning designer with 15+ years of experience helping students discover their potential. Craig believes the best learning happens when students are challenged to solve real problems, ask big questions, and work together. His GT Anywhere workshops combine hands-on experiences with meaningful challenges that inspire curiosity, confidence, and lifelong learning.

items.title: The Caldera
items.desc: Become a Yellowstone field scientist and investigate real wildlife, geothermal, or volcanic data.

items.title: Glitch and Grow
items.desc: Choose a difficult skill, document every failure, and prove measurable improvement through deliberate practice.

items.title: Going Viral
items.desc: Create original content, use AI feedback to improve it, and learn how creators capture attention.

items.title: The Greenlight
items.desc: Write a professional script, create an AI-animated short film, and pitch it to a real film-industry pro.

items.title: Venture Lab
items.desc: Launch a real digital business, earn revenue, and pitch your venture to adult judges.

items.title: History on Trial
items.desc: Research a historical topic you love using primary sources and present at the National History Day competition.

name: David Zook
bio: Has coached robotics teams (winning multiple world championships), taught cars to drive themselves at Tesla, founded a high school, and raised two genuinely gifted kids who keep him appropriately humble. He's spent his career in rooms full of smart people trying to do hard things. When he's not at GT, he's probably on a plane, at a table with exceptional food, or recruiting another smart person into his orbit.

items.title: AI Robot Coach Academy
items.desc: Coach robots through complex VEX VR challenges using Claude and ChatGPT, documented in an Engineering Notebook.
items.monoCover: Sciences AI Robot Coach Academy GT Anywhere AR

items.title: Future Champion Lab
items.desc: Become a competitive analyst — study how champion teams win, and identify the patterns that produce winners.
items.monoCover: Sciences Future Champion Lab GT Anywhere FC

items.title: VEX All-Stars: Rebuild the Legends
items.desc: A virtual robotics challenge: compete on legendary VEX fields with Claude as your AI engineering partner.
items.monoCover: Sciences VEX All-Stars: Rebuild the Legends GT Anywhere VA

items.title: Japanese Black Belt: Reverse-Engineering a Language
items.desc: Reverse-engineer how Japanese verbs work so you can predict words you've never seen — drills, partner talk, rapid-fire.
items.monoCover: Humanities Japanese Black Belt: Reverse-Engineering a Language GT Anywhere JB

items.title: Botball Robotics Team: Become a Founding Member
items.desc: Found GT's first competition robotics team — fully autonomous robots, building toward the Botball World Championship.
items.monoCover: Competition Botball Robotics Team: Become a Founding Member GT Anywhere BR

name: Melissa Muir
bio: M.A.T. — a bilingual educator with 15+ years across public, private, online, and homeschool classrooms, raising four trilingual kids in Quito. She takes every opportunity to get kids reading and surprising themselves with what they can do.

items.title: The Unbuyable Product Lab
items.desc: Invent a product that doesn't exist, 3D-print it, and pitch it to builders from Apple, Google, and SayMake.

items.title: Codebreakers
items.desc: Crack secret codes that get harder every week — including a final one no one has ever solved.

items.title: Page Turners
items.desc: Read what you love, score points for going bold into new genres, and climb the leaderboard.

items.title: Soy un Experto
items.desc: Become the expert on Ecuadorian food and give a live tour — all in Spanish. The best guides earn a trip to Quito.

items.title: Literary League
items.desc: Divisions, standings, and real bragging rights for reading bravely and hitting your number.

name: Norberto Troncoso
bio: A coach of hundreds of national and state champions, international keynote speaker, and creator of the P.O.W.E.R. Framework, Norberto teaches students that courage isn't the absence of fear, it's doing it scared. He helps kids build fearless communication, emotional intelligence, and leadership identity so they speak up, lead themselves, and rise to any room they walk into.

items.title: One Idea
items.desc: Find one original idea you believe in, build a TEDx-style talk around it, and deliver it live.
items.monoCover: Humanities One Idea GT Anywhere OI

items.title: Say That Again
items.desc: Draw a random topic cold, take a clear position, and defend it under live challenge. No notes. No prep.
items.monoCover: Humanities Say That Again GT Anywhere ST

items.title: The Deal
items.desc: Master the Ackerman negotiation model used by FBI negotiators and close a real deal at a local business.
items.monoCover: Humanities The Deal GT Anywhere TD

items.title: I Said What I Said
items.desc: Pick a side, say it clearly with the Point-Reason frame, and hold your ground when a peer pushes back.
items.monoCover: Competition I Said What I Said GT Anywhere IS

items.title: Become the Character
items.desc: Cut a published script to a solo 10-minute dramatic interpretation and perform live for theater pros (NSDA criteria).
items.monoCover: Competition Become the Character GT Anywhere BT

items.title: The Verdict
items.desc: Build a real case, cross-examine witnesses, and argue before outside judges who deliver a binding verdict.
items.monoCover: Competition The Verdict GT Anywhere TV

name: Ruchi Shukla
bio: An educator for 15 years, working in school systems around the world. She builds workshops that help kids learn about important global issues through hands-on play and activities.

items.title: Board Game Masters
items.desc: Build strategic thinking and confidence through learning, practicing, and improving at board games.

items.title: Board Game Designer
items.desc: Design, playtest, refine, and pitch an original board game while learning strategy and game-design thinking.

items.title: Food Lab Challenge
items.desc: Become a food scientist and recipe creator — explore flavor, nutrition, kitchen skills, and food science.

items.title: Passport Mission
items.desc: Explore countries through hands-on cultural experiences, building global awareness and curiosity.

items.title: Toy Inventors
items.desc: Turn recycled materials into playable toys while learning to invent, test, and improve ideas.

items.title: Global Host Challenge
items.desc: Investigate cultural norms, interview people from other backgrounds, and design a cultural experience.

items.title: Sky Tower Challenge
items.desc: Design, test, and rebuild towers — balancing height, strength, and stability like a real engineer.

items.title: Young Inventors Challenge
items.desc: Invent, prototype, test, and pitch creative solutions using recycled materials — real design thinking.

name: Sarah Langdon
bio: An educator, advisor, and learning designer who believes students rise to the level of authentic challenge. She creates GT Anywhere workshops that blend academic rigor with real-world projects, helping kids discover they're capable of more than they imagined.

items.title: Attractions in Action
items.desc: Design an original attraction, prove the guest-flow and budget math, and field-test it on an earned Disney trip.

items.title: Deep Time
items.desc: Learn to read a mountain like a book, then read a never-before-seen rock face aloud to a working geologist.

items.title: Mind Lab
items.desc: Design and run an original behavioral-science experiment on real people, then defend it to a working psychologist.

items.title: Change Makers
items.desc: Write an evidence-based policy letter to your council member, then defend it in live testimony under expert Q&A.

items.title: On the Record
items.desc: Report, interview, and produce a real podcast episode, then publish it to a live audience.

items.title: Rewrite History
items.desc: Become a real historical figure and fight to change how history ends — winning the room with primary-source arguments.

name: Yash Mehta
bio: International Olympiad Medallist, with a decade of experience selecting, training, and mentoring national teams to top ranks at International Olympiads. He coaches with the philosophy that problem-solving is the most fundamental skill one must be equipped with.

items.title: Math Competitor Academy
items.desc: Students train like math athletes, mastering competition strategies, tackling challenging problems, and competing in AMC 8, MOEMS, Math Kangaroo, and MathCounts.
items.calendar: Year-long · Math Kangaroo / MOEMS / AMC 8 / MathCounts

items.title: Math Elite Academy
items.desc: Students tackle advanced competition mathematics, train for AMC 8, MathCounts, AMC 10, and AIME pathways, and prove their skills in nationally recognized contests.
items.calendar: Year-long · AMC 8 / MathCounts / AMC 10 / AIME pathway
